paragraph: New Milford's single-family market is moving at a competitive clip. Forty-seven homes sold over the last six months at a median of $680,000, with about half of all sales landing above asking price. The sale-to-list ratio sits right at 1.00, meaning homes are selling for essentially their listed price on average, and the median days on market of 15 days tells you that priced homes are moving without extended waits. You're seeing enough activity that multiple offers aren't rare, particularly on homes in the $600,000 to $750,000 range where inventory turns over fastest. The outliers are real too. The market spans from $501,000 to $1,900,000, so while New Milford has a solid mid-market core, there's genuine breadth for buyers working at different price points. If you're selling, you have some leverage to hold firm on price if the home shows well. If you're buying, the best homes will still draw competition, but patience and a realistic list price relative to comparable sales will get you results faster than waiting for a softer market.
